calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Dec-01-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Emilie Calhoun, via ZOOM, for Deirdre Nesbit-Combs, Administrator.

Ms. Calhoun offers advocacy for her request for approval of extraordinary fees including searching for an attorney in Ireland and the complications that this matte presented. The court then points to the second issue emphasized by the Examiner. Ms. Calhoun confirms that Examiner's item number two regarding fees owed to the previous attorney being made payable to the trustee of the deceased attorney's trust is no longer an issue.

The Accounting Petition is submitted.

Judge: Hon. Ross Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Aug-29-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Emilie Calhoun for and with Deirdre Nesbit-Combs.

The Court noted that notice has been given to the Public Administrator and that they now have a nomination. Ms. Calhoun stated that there was no response by the Public Administrator and that she is not aware of any objections. The Court granted the Petition for Letters of Administration (subject to review of the order). A minimum bond of $10,000.00 is required. Ms. Calhoun questions bond stating that there are no assets. The Court stated there are potential claims and there are assets coming in. Status on close of estate is set for March 3, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Gomez. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Aug-15-2018 Continued To Aug-29-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Jul-18-2018 Continued To Aug-15-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Elina Protich for and with Deirdre Nesbit-Combs.

The Court questions whether Ms. Nesbit-Combs has served as Administrator in other cases and if so, whether it requires notice to the Public Administrator as Ms. Nesbit-Combs is lower in priority. Ms. Protich stated that Ms. Nesbit-Combs has served on other cases on many occasions, that she has priority as a Creditor, that Ms. Nesbit-Combs hired an Attorney in Ireland and was advised that things would run smoother if they had a probate here, and that the Decedent has other Creditors in California that need to be paid as well. The Court noted that a Creditor is still lower in priority. The Petition for Letters of Administration is continued to August 15, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for notice to the Public Administrator. Any supplements as to why this is a good idea is to be filed August 9, 2018.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.
